response.justification: The candidate is a skilled polyglot engineer with strong craftsmanship practices, but he does not meet the core mandatory requirements. His professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the required 5-10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. His database background is MySQL and MongoDB rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no fixed income, capital markets, or investment banking domain exposure — RIDE Capital was a Node/TypeScript fintech role, not capital markets systems work. The profile is a mismatch for this consulting engagement.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O microservices handling millions of requests per second)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and testing tooling authorship
Broad polyglot background with long tenure and demonstrated communication skills (workshops, talks, courses)
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience versus the mandatory 5-10 years on Windows
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; SQL background is limited to MySQL/MongoDB
No fixed income, derivatives, capital markets, or investment banking domain knowledge

key_factors.factor: No fixed income or capital markets domain exposure
Transferable low-latency systems and strong engineering fundamentals
